skill.name: longbridge-watchlist
skill.description: Manage Longbridge watchlist groups, price alerts, and community stock lists through the Longbridge CLI with explicit confirmation before changes.
skill.summary: Manage Longbridge watchlists, alerts, and community stock lists with confirmation gates.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external-command patterns because the skill documents Longbridge CLI usage. The CLI usage is core functionality and includes explicit confirmation before mutations, but it can persistently modify account watchlists, alerts, and sharelists. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, weak cryptography, or system reconnaissance evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands for watchlists, alerts, and sharelists. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it gives the assistant a command execution surface and can change account data after user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced files directly instruct use of longbridge CLI commands. Context shows these commands are expected product functionality, not hidden shell execution.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Account-State Mutation
medium_findings.description: The skill can create, rename, delete, add, remove, enable, disable, and sort Longbridge watchlist, alert, and sharelist resources. The files require preview and explicit confirmation, which reduces risk, but the changes are persistent in the user account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe persistent mutating operations and the mandatory confirmation flow. No evidence shows trading or fund movement.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL
low_findings.description: The network findings point to a GitHub URL for installing longbridge-terminal. This is a documentation link, not a command that transmits local data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hardcoded URL appears in user-facing error handling text. I found no evidence of automated network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography and low reconnaissance alerts appear to be scanner matches inside normal prose and command examples. No cryptographic code, host fingerprinting, secret collection, or reconnaissance workflow was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown instructions about confirmations, login, or normal skill behavior. They do not contain cryptographic primitives or system reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown instructions contain longbridge CLI invocations that an agent may execute. These commands should remain bound to the documented preview and confirmation flow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and actionable. Their intent is legitimate, but execution affects external Longbridge state.

content.value_statement: Investors need a reliable way to organize Longbridge watchlists, alerts, and community stock lists. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge CLI workflows with confirmation before changes.
actual_capabilities: Lists Longbridge watchlist groups and retrieves group identifiers.
Creates, renames, deletes, and updates watchlist groups after confirmation.
Lists, creates, deletes, enables, and disables Longbridge price alerts.
Browses popular community stock lists and shows list details.
Creates and manages community stock lists after explicit confirmation.
Provides bilingual handling for Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English prompts.
limitations: Requires the Longbridge CLI for command-based workflows.
Requires Longbridge login for account-specific and write operations.
Does not place trades or manage orders.
Depends on current Longbridge CLI flags and account permissions.

use_cases.title: Organize Personal Watchlists
use_cases.description: Create groups, add symbols, remove stale symbols, and rename lists in a Longbridge account.

use_cases.title: Maintain Price Alerts
use_cases.description: List existing alerts and prepare confirmed changes for price thresholds and alert status.

use_cases.title: Publish Stock Collections
use_cases.description: Browse, create, update, and sort public stock lists that other Longbridge users can follow.

prompt_templates.title: List My Watchlists
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Longbridge watchlist groups and the symbols in each group.

prompt_templates.title: Add Symbols With Confirmation
prompt_templates.prompt: Add NVDA.US and AAPL.US to my Longbridge technology watchlist. Preview the change before doing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Review Alert Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: List my Longbridge price alerts for TSLA.US and help me decide which ones should be disabled.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Community List
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Longbridge sharelists, then prepare a confirmed update to sort one list by my preferred symbol order.

output_examples.input: Add AAPL.US to my Growth group.
output_examples.output: The skill previews the exact group and symbol, asks for confirmation, then runs the Longbridge update only after consent.
The skill returns a table with list names, owners, subscriber counts, symbol counts, and Longbridge as the source.

output_examples.input: Set an alert for NVDA.US at 200 when it rises.
output_examples.output: The skill confirms the symbol, price, and direction before creating the alert in the Longbridge account.

best_practices: Use read-only list commands before mutating a watchlist, alert, or sharelist.
Confirm group IDs, alert IDs, symbols, prices, and directions before approving execution.
Keep the Longbridge CLI current and check help output when flags are unclear.
anti_patterns: Do not approve vague requests such as reorganizing lists without a concrete action.
Do not bypass the preview and confirmation step for any account change.
Do not use this skill for trading, order placement, or investment advice.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It manages watchlists, alerts, and community stock lists. It does not place orders or move funds.

faq.question: Do write operations require confirmation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires a preview and explicit user confirmation before any persistent change.

faq.question: What login is required?
faq.answer: Read-only public sharelist browsing may not require login. Account-specific and write operations require Longbridge login.

faq.question: Can it work without the Longbridge CLI?
faq.answer: The documented workflow uses the Longbridge CLI. The skill mentions MCP fallback only when available at runtime.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: It is designed to respond in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English based on the user prompt.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: It uses external commands and can change persistent account data, but it includes confirmation gates and no malicious behavior was found.
